people_highlights.name: Sam Altman
people_highlights.post:
you know what

all of these "which is better" polls are silly

use codex or claude code, whatever works best for you

i am grateful we live in a time with such amazing tools, and grateful there is a choice

people_highlights.name: OpenAI
people_highlights.post:
One week since the launch of GPT-5.5, and it’s already our strongest model launch yet. 

API revenue is growing more than 2x faster than any prior release, while Codex doubled revenue in under seven days as enterprise demand for agentic coding tools keeps climbing.

people_highlights.name: François Chollet
people_highlights.post:
AI automates tasks, not jobs, and when a task gets cheaper, demand for the job grows. 

AI cannot automate jobs end-to-end because it lacks autonomy and cannot operate without supervision. There is still zero job from 2022 that can be performed end-to-end by AI, not even
people_highlights.why: Recent post from Keras creator at Google

people_highlights.name: Anthropic
people_highlights.post: In new Anthropic Fellows research, we discuss “introspection adapters": a tool that allows language models to self-report behaviors they've learned during training—including potential misalignment.

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post:
"Load bearing," "I keep coming back to," "Not X, but Y" 

A curse of using AI a lot is that you realize how much of the writing around you is just AI, now

People who don't use AI have been unable to identify AI prose on sight, but those who use it a lot can spot the tells easily
people_highlights.why: Recent post from AI researcher at Wharton

What makes this announcement so interesting is that it gives AI tools direct, authorized access to help manage your Meta Ads account through natural
